Cursor.description: Opaque base64 cursor from a previous response.
Cursor.name: cursor

IdempotencyKey.description: UUIDv4. Makes mutating requests safe to retry / offline-replay. Accepted on all mutating operations for a uniform client contract; on naturally-idempotent operations (DELETE, login) the key is accepted but inert.
IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key

TenantHeader.description: DEPRECATED transitional header. Tenant identity will come from the JWT tenant_id claim once auth middleware lands; this header is the interim placeholder and must not be relied upon in production.
TenantHeader.name: X-Tenant-ID

Conflict.description: Conflict (unique constraint or idempotency-key reuse with different payload)

bcea_hours_compliant.description: Whether daily hours stayed within BCEA limits.

break_compliant.description: Whether statutory meal/rest breaks were observed.

ClockingEventRequest.description: All presence-evidence fields are TRUST INPUTS, scored by the trust engine — not gates.

accuracy.description: GPS accuracy in metres; very low accuracy lowers trust.

lat.description: GPS latitude. Geofence checked via PostGIS; outside → flagged + lower score, NOT rejected.

qr_payload.description: Signed single-use QR (KLOKA|base64|HMAC). Freshness is a score input; a forged signature hard-rejects.

ClockingEvidence.description: Structured presence evidence. All fields are TRUST INPUTS scored by the trust engine, not gates (except a forged QR signature, which hard-rejects upstream).

device_fingerprint.description: Raw device fingerprint at clock time.

photo_url.description: Stored capture for async face match.

qr.description: QR proof-of-presence freshness metadata. The signed payload itself is on the parent request's qr_payload.

registered.description: Whether the presenting device is a registered RCID.

dispute_id.description: Optional link to the dispute that prompted this adjustment (audit trail).

CreateDisputeRequest.description: Must reference either an exception_id or an attendance_date.

reason.example: I was on site but the geofence flagged me as absent.

worker_id.description: Admin-only; defaults to the authenticated worker when omitted.

condition.description: Rule-engine condition expression. Server-side parsed against an allow-listed grammar.

CreateShiftRequest.description: Shift template. If end_time is earlier than start_time the shift is treated as crossing midnight.

tenant_key.description: Shown ONCE. Store securely; sent as X-Tenant-Key on login. Cannot be retrieved again — only rotated.

consent_timestamp.description: When biometric consent was captured (POPIA audit).

date_of_birth.description: Required for BCEA night-work / young-worker rules.

include_biometrics.description: Whether to include biometric templates/metadata in the export.

worker_id.description: Data subject whose data is exported.

attestation_token.description: Opaque platform attestation token. Never logged.

signals.description: Raw platform verdict fields (e.g. basicIntegrity, ctsProfileMatch).

verdict.description: TRUST INPUT to clocking scores, not a hard gate.

E164Phone.description: South African mobile number in E.164 format. Always +27 followed by a mobile prefix (6, 7, or 8) and 8 further digits — 12 characters total. Local 0-prefixed numbers (e.g. 0821234567) must be normalised to +27 before submission.

photo_url.description: Object-store URL of the uploaded enrollment photo. Raw biometric never sent in-band.

session_token.description: Limited challenge token. Only completes MFA; not a full access token.

NotificationPreferences.description: POPIA-compliant channel opt-ins. All default to false until the worker opts in.

OpaqueToken.description: Opaque server-issued token. Treated as bearer material; never logged.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page; null at end.

PayrollReportRequest.description: Generate a payroll export. period_end must be on or after period_start (validated server-side; 422 otherwise).

Pin.description: 4-8 digit numeric PIN. Security derives from device binding and rate limiting, not PIN entropy. Write-only; never returned.

detail.example: phone must be a valid South African mobile number (+27[6-8]xxxxxxxx)

errors.description: Per-field validation errors (present on 400 validation failures).

token.description: Provider push token. Treated as sensitive; never logged.

refresh_token.description: Opaque refresh token. Optional for non-browser clients; browsers send the HttpOnly cookie instead.

resolution.example: Upheld; CCTV confirms presence. Attendance corrected.

TokenPair.description: User JWT pair. The refresh token is delivered ONLY via an HttpOnly Secure cookie (Set-Cookie), never in this body, to prevent XSS theft.

access_token.description: User JWT (signed with the tenant secret). Sent as Bearer on all endpoints.

credential.description: PublicKeyCredential attestation, base64url-encoded per WebAuthn L2.

secret.description: Signing secret, shown once at creation.

biometric_consent.description: POPIA consent to biometric (NFR) processing. False disables face recognition.

consent_timestamp.description: When biometric consent was captured (POPIA audit).

date_of_birth.description: Required for BCEA night-work / young-worker rules.

platformKey.description: Kloka-internal platform credential. Required to create tenants.
platformKey.name: X-Platform-Key

tenantKey.description: Per-tenant API key, returned ONCE when the tenant is created. Scopes the login call to a tenant. This is NOT the JWT signing secret — that never leaves the server. This key only identifies + authorises the tenant context.
tenantKey.name: X-Tenant-Key

info.description: Full API surface (88 paths, 109 operations) for the Kloka zero-trust workforce attendance platform. Generated from the locked API_DESIGN.md. Schemas are typed where the domain is built (clocking, auth) and generic objects where the domain is still designed; tighten per-domain as code lands.
info.title: Kloka Attendance Platform API

post.summary: Admin login step 1 (email + password) scoped by tenant key — returns MFA challenge

get.description: Returns the complete authenticated user state (identity, tenant, sites, bound devices, active assignments, notification preferences) in a single call for instant native app onboarding or PWA-to-native migration.
get.summary: Bootstrap full user state for native onboarding/migration

post.description: Validates a platform attestation token (Play Integrity, Huawei Safety Detect, or Apple DeviceCheck). The verdict is a TRUST INPUT to clocking scores, not a hard gate.
Secure-migration helper. Revokes every device bound to the caller except the device making the request (identified by its bound key / current session).
post.summary: Revoke all bound devices except the current one
Step 2: OTP + session token, returns JWT pair

post.description: Verifies the SMS OTP and sets the new PIN. PIN is hashed with bcrypt over hex(pin_salt) + user_salt + pin server-side.
post.summary: Confirm worker PIN reset with OTP

post.description: Proves IDENTITY only (no QR/face/geofence — those belong to clocking). Requires the X-Tenant-Key header to scope the tenant. On success returns a user JWT pair; the access token is used as Bearer on all subsequent endpoints. The JWT is signed with the tenant's server-side secret and carries tenant_id + role as claims.

423.description: Account locked (rate limit / too many attempts)

post.summary: Worker login (phone + PIN) scoped by tenant key — returns user JWT

post.description: Triggers an SMS OTP to the worker's registered E.164 phone for PIN recovery. Always returns 202 to avoid leaking which numbers are enrolled.

202.description: OTP dispatched if the number is enrolled

post.summary: Request worker PIN reset (sends SMS OTP)

has_more.description: True if more pages exist (next_cursor != null).

post.description: Proves PRESENCE, not identity (the worker is already authenticated). GPS, QR, facial biometric and geofence are EVIDENCE attached to this event and scored by the trust engine — NOT pass/fail gates. Per ADR 0005: outside-geofence is recorded, flagged and scored lower (not rejected); QR freshness is a score input. Only categorical failures hard-reject: forged QR signature, or unbound device at a QR-required site. Server created_at is authoritative; device timestamp is evidence only.

422.description: Categorical rejection (forged QR / unbound device at QR-required site)

get.description: SSE stream. Each event is a JSON-encoded attendance update.

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream of live attendance

post.description: Revokes a worker's biometric processing consent under POPIA. Disables native face recognition (NFR) for that worker; subsequent clocking falls back to non-biometric evidence.
Queues a full data subject export (POPIA/PAIA). Returns an async Job; the export is delivered via the report download flow when ready.
post.summary: Trigger a POPIA/PAIA data subject export

has_more.description: True if more pages exist (next_cursor != null).
True if more pages exist (next_cursor != null).

patch.summary: Approve, reject, or cancel a leave request

post.summary: Create a tenant and issue its one-time tenant key
